name: Microsoft Intune
description: Cloud-based endpoint management platform for Windows, macOS, iOS and Android. Yields device compliance policy reports, configuration profile status, encryption (BitLocker/FileVault) reports and enrolled-device inventory, exportable via the admin centre or the Microsoft Graph API.

steps.action: Sign in to the Microsoft Intune admin centre (intune.microsoft.com) and navigate to Reports > Device compliance > Reports tab > Device compliance, then select Generate report
steps.permissions_required: Read Only Operator (Intune built-in role) or Intune Administrator
steps.security_note: Use a least-privilege Read Only Operator account for evidence collection, not a Global Administrator

steps.action: Select Export to download the device compliance report as CSV; repeat for Reports > Device compliance > Reports tab > Noncompliant devices and settings
steps.audit_note: Exports preserve any filters applied on screen — record the filters used, and save files with date-stamped names

steps.action: Navigate to Devices > Monitor > Encryption report and select Export to capture BitLocker/FileVault encryption status for all managed devices
steps.audit_note: The encryption report covers both Windows BitLocker and macOS FileVault, including encryption readiness and OS drive encryption state

steps.action: Navigate to Devices > All devices and select Export to download the enrolled-device inventory, including compliance state and last check-in time
steps.audit_note: The inventory export is the completeness baseline — compliance evidence should reconcile against this device count

steps.action: Upload the exported CSV files to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference and collection date
steps.security_note: Exports contain device names, serial numbers and user principal names — handle per your data classification policy

steps.action: In the Microsoft Intune admin centre, navigate to Reports > Intune Data warehouse > Data warehouse and select Get Power BI App to install the Intune Compliance (Data Warehouse) app
In Power BI, open the app's semantic model settings and enable Scheduled refresh so compliance data is refreshed automatically
steps.audit_note: The Intune Data Warehouse itself refreshes daily; align the Power BI refresh schedule with it so reports never serve stale data

steps.action: Intune has no native scheduled email report — use Power BI report subscriptions (Subscribe to report) to deliver a weekly compliance snapshot to the compliance team mailbox
steps.security_note: Restrict subscription recipients and Power BI workspace access to authorised compliance personnel only

steps.action: Each cycle, export the delivered compliance report pages to PDF or CSV and file them in the evidence repository
steps.audit_note: Retain the generation timestamp shown on the report so the evidence period is verifiable

steps.action: Quarterly, verify the Data Warehouse connection and refresh history to confirm no missed refresh cycles
steps.audit_note: Power BI refresh history provides an audit trail that the reporting pipeline ran on schedule

steps.action: Register an application in the Microsoft Entra admin centre (Identity > Applications > App registrations), grant it the application permissions DeviceManagementManagedDevices.Read.All and DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All, and grant admin consent
steps.permissions_required: Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator (to grant admin consent)
steps.security_note: Use certificate credentials or a managed identity rather than a client secret where possible; scope permissions read-only

steps.action: Call POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/deviceManagement/reports/exportJobs with reportName DeviceCompliance (and NonCompliantDevicesAndSettings) to create report export jobs
Poll GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/deviceManagement/reports/exportJobs('{id}') until status is completed, then download the zipped CSV from the returned URL
steps.audit_note: The exportJobs API is throttled at 100 requests per tenant per minute — poll with back-off to avoid dropped runs

steps.action: Call GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/deviceManagement/managedDevices to capture enrolled-device inventory, including complianceState and isEncrypted per device
steps.audit_note: isEncrypted per managed device corroborates the BitLocker/FileVault encryption report

steps.action: Schedule the collection daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, writing outputs to the evidence repository with run timestamp and job IDs
steps.security_note: Store the app credential in a secrets manager (e.g. Azure Key Vault), never in plaintext configuration, and rotate on a defined schedule

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed runs, and validate each export's device count against the previous run to detect silent gaps
steps.audit_note: Row-count validation against the enrolled-device baseline provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Intune Managed Evidence Pipeline via Azure Monitor

steps.action: In the Microsoft Intune admin centre, navigate to Reports > Diagnostics settings and route AuditLogs, OperationalLogs, DeviceComplianceOrg and IntuneDevices to a Log Analytics workspace and/or Event Hub for SIEM consumption
steps.permissions_required: Intune Administrator (Microsoft Entra role); Log Analytics Contributor on the workspace
steps.security_note: Restrict access to the destination workspace/Event Hub — the streams contain device and user identifiers

steps.action: Note that Microsoft Graph change notifications do not support deviceManagement/managedDevices, so there is no push webhook for device compliance — continuous collection is achieved via the Azure Monitor export combined with the scheduled Graph exportJobs collection from L3

Design for the documented export behaviour: DeviceComplianceOrg and IntuneDevices data is exported once per 24-hour period with up to 48 hours latency and may contain up to 100% duplicated records — deduplicate on ingestion and never assume a fixed delivery time
steps.audit_note: Documenting and handling the vendor's stated latency and duplication behaviour is itself evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Configure completeness metrics: reconcile daily IntuneDevices record counts against the enrolled-device inventory, and alert when a 24-hour export window passes with no DeviceComplianceOrg data
steps.audit_note: Gap detection against the enrolment baseline demonstrates collection completeness to auditors

steps.action: Build an Azure Monitor workbook or SIEM dashboard showing collection health: records per day, compliance rate trend, encryption coverage and failed export windows
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of process health

steps.action: Automate a quarterly collection-health report summarising export gaps, duplicate rates and SLA breaches, and file it in the evidence repository
